description:
You are a security engineer and must quickly rotate hard-coded credentials that have been discovered in two configuration files of a small internal application.  
  Everything is located in your normal, non-privileged account under “/home/user”.

  1. Configuration files to update  
     • YAML file: /home/user/app/config.yaml  
       Current excerpt (do not remove other keys, just update the listed one):  
       database:  
         user: appuser  
         password: changeme123  

     • TOML file: /home/user/app/secret.toml  
       Current excerpt:  
       [api]  
       token = "changeme123"  

  2. Required new secrets  
     • New database password: S3cur3P@ssw0rd!  
     • New API token: S3cur3Tok3n!  

  3. Audit trail  
     After the update, create a single-line log file at /home/user/logs/cred_rotation.log.  
     The file must contain exactly one line in this precise format (including the final full stop, no extra spaces or line breaks):  

     2024-01-01T00:00:00Z Credentials rotated successfully.

     Automated tests will fail if the timestamp, spacing, capitalisation, or punctuation differ in any way, or if additional lines are present.

  4. Constraints  
     • Accomplish the entire credential rotation with one shell command of your choice (e.g. a properly crafted sed invocation).  
     • Do not require sudo; stay inside your user’s home directory.  
     • Leave all other content in each configuration file unchanged.  
     • Ensure both the YAML and TOML files reflect the new secrets exactly as given above, with no trailing spaces.  
     • Make sure /home/user/logs/cred_rotation.log is created with the required single line.

  When finished, you can output the command you used and display the resulting file snippets to confirm the change, but the key deliverable is that the files on disk match the specification so the automated checker succeeds.
truth:
Initial state that will exist before the agent starts:  

  1. /home/user/app/config.yaml  
     ---
     app_name: sampleapp
     database:
       user: appuser
       password: changeme123
     logging:
       level: info
     ---

  2. /home/user/app/secret.toml  
     # Internal secrets
     [api]
     token = "changeme123"

  3. /home/user/logs directory already exists and is writable by the current user; no cred_rotation.log file is present.

  Expected final state after successful agent execution:

  1. /home/user/app/config.yaml must contain line  
       password: S3cur3P@ssw0rd!  
     and must NOT contain the string “changeme123”. All other lines remain exactly as originally.

  2. /home/user/app/secret.toml must contain line  
       token = "S3cur3Tok3n!"  
     and must NOT contain the string “changeme123”. All other lines remain exactly as originally.

  3. /home/user/logs/cred_rotation.log exists and its content is exactly (including newline at EOF):  
     2024-01-01T00:00:00Z Credentials rotated successfully.\n
name: task_000000_c1cfac98
